filters.q: =("application submitted" OR "submitted successfully" OR interview OR unfortunately OR "we regret" OR "thank you for applying" OR "than you for your interest" OR "next steps" OR offer OR hired OR "not moving forward" OR "other candidates")

parameters.text:
=You are a JSON-only extraction API. Respond with ONLY a raw JSON object.
No explanations. No markdown. No backticks. No preamble. Just raw JSON.

Analyze the email below and return this exact structure:
{
  "isJobRelated": true or false,
  "company": "company name or null",
  "role": "job title or null",
  "status": "Applied|Interviewing|Offer|Rejected|Ghosted|N/A",
  "dateReceived": "YYYY-MM-DD or null",
  "notes": "one sentence summary or null",
  "jobPoster": "LinkedIn|Indeed|Jobstreet|Workday|Company Website|Other|N/A"
}

CRITICAL RULES:
- "isJobRelated" must ALWAYS be true or false — never null, never omitted
- All other fields must always be present in the response — use null or "N/A" as defaults, never omit a field
- If email content is missing or unclear, default isJobRelated to false and all other fields to null/"N/A"

STEP 1 — Decide isJobRelated.

Set TRUE only if the email is about a SPECIFIC job the user personally applied to:
- Application confirmation ("We received your application", "Thank you for applying")
- Interview invitation, scheduling, or reminder
- Assessment or test invitation tied to a specific application
- A job offer
- A rejection or regret notice for a role applied to
- A recruiter or hiring manager personally following up about a specific application

Set FALSE for everything else:
- Job alert digests or "jobs matching your search" emails
- "X new jobs at companies you follow" or recommendation emails
- Promotional emails, newsletters, career tips, webinar invites
- Emails the user sent (outbound)
- Generic marketing not tied to a specific application
- Empty or unreadable emails

STEP 2 — Extract fields.

If isJobRelated is TRUE:
- company: actual hiring company from From field or body — NOT the ATS platform. Example: "Workday Analog Devices <...@myworkday.com>" → "Analog Devices"
- role: exact job title from Subject, strip requisition numbers and fill dates. Example: "Thank you for applying - R262903 Associate Embedded Software Engineer (Fill Date: 06/22/2026)" → "Associate Embedded Software Engineer"
- status:
  - "Applied" = confirmation only, no decision yet
  - "Interviewing" = interview invite, scheduling, or assessment
  - "Offer" = explicit offer extended
  - "Rejected" = rejection language present ("we regret", "after careful consideration", "not selected", "unfortunately", "other candidates")
  - "Ghosted" = email explicitly states search is paused or closed — never infer from silence
- dateReceived: dateStamp converted to YYYY-MM-DD, stripping the time portion
- notes: one short factual sentence (e.g. "Application rejected after review for Associate Embedded Software Engineer role")
- jobPoster:
  - myworkday.com → "Workday"
  - linkedin.com → "LinkedIn"
  - indeed.com → "Indeed"
  - jobstreet.com → "Jobstreet"
  - greenhouse.io → "Greenhouse"
  - lever.co → "Lever"
  - company's own domain → "Company Website"
  - anything else → "Other"

If isJobRelated is FALSE:
- company, role, notes → null
- status → "N/A"
- jobPoster → "N/A"
- dateReceived → dateStamp converted to YYYY-MM-DD

KEY DISTINCTION: The From field often shows the ATS platform paired with the real company name — always extract the actual employer for "company", the platform goes in "jobPoster".

Email Subject: {{ $json.Subject }}
Email From: {{ $json.From }}
Email Date: {{ $json.dateStamp }}
Email Body: {{ $json.snippet }}

parameters.query:
INSERT INTO job_applications (
  id, company, role, status, date_received, notes, job_poster, updated_at
)
VALUES (
  '{{ $json.id }}',
  '{{ $json.company }}',
  '{{ $json.role }}',
  '{{ $json.status }}',
  '{{ $json['Date Received'] }}',
  '{{ $json.notes }}',
  '{{ $json.jobPoster }}',
  CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
)
ON CONFLICT (id)
DO UPDATE SET
  status = EXCLUDED.status,
  notes = EXCLUDED.notes,
  updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP;
